California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
March 19, 199 0
AIan W. Courtney
567 san Nicolas Drive, suite 408
Newport Beach, cA 92660
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-9o-o94
Dear Mr. Courtney:
This is in response to your request for advice regardinq the
responsibilities of George Hanna, foroer candi.date for-Mayor of
santa Ana, under the PoliticaL Reform Act (the rrActrr).r
OUESTION
Does a settlement agreement, entered into by George llanna to
pay a debt olred by the Friends of George Hanna, a control-Ied
committee, to Arnotd Steinberg and Associates ('rSteinberg'r)
vi.olate the PoliticaL Refon0 Act?
CONCLUS ION
The terms of the settlenent agreement do not violate the
Political Reform Act.
FACTS
ceorge Hanna was a candidate for mayor of santa Ana in the
November, 1988, general election. 
In september and october of
1988, the Friends of George Hanna conmittee obtained tvro public
opj.nj.on po11s from steinberg. steinberg bi1led the committee
99,5oo for these polls.
The corn'nittee became insoLvent and l-acked sufficient funds to
pay for these po11s after George Hanna lost the election. 
On
Auglrst 7 , 19A9, Steinberg filed a Municipal Court lawsuit against
George Hanna to collect the $9,500, plus interest, costs and
1^ Government code Sections 8l-0oo-91015. All statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regTulations appear at 2 Californj.a Code of ReguLatj.ons Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Tit1e 2
Division 6 of the Cali.fornia code of Regulations.
428 J street, Suite 8oo o P.o. Box 8oz o sacramento cA 958o4-o8o? o (9 16) 3 zz-s66o


Our Fi■e No. A-90-094
Page 2
attorney's fees.
the lawsuit.
The conmittee was not naned as a defendant in
ceorge llanna answered the complaint by generally denying the
allegations and asserted various affirnative defenses. The case
was set for trial 
on January 22, L989 , at which time the following
settlement agreement was reached between George Hanna and
steinberg in open court:
1. The comrnittee agreed to conduct a fund raising effort
within 90 days.
2. ceorge Hanna personally guaranteed that a minimum of
$2,oOO would be paid to Steinberg if less than $2,000 is raised.
3. If more than S2,oo0 is raised, Steinberg is to receive
all of the noney raised, up to a maxj-mum of 511,500. Anything
over $11,500 would be kept by the conmittee to retire other debts
fron the election.
4. The coumittee's former campaign consultant, Frank
Caternichio (who originally ordered the surveys ' who lras present
in court, and consented to this arrangeuentl , will supply the
corn'nittee rrith a nailing Iist of contributors and assist in the
fund raising effort. 
For this, Mr. caternichio will be paid a
commission of 58 of all sums raised in excess of $11,500.
5. ceorge Hanna will personafl,y pay for all roailing costs
associated hrith the fund raising effort as the committee has no
money at this time.
6. AII funds wouLd be raised in the name of the coumittee
and will be deposited into its checking account.
7. Upon paltment to steinberg under the terBs of this
settleDent, the lawsuit would be dj.snissed with prejudice.
ANALYSIS
Mr. Hanna has agreed to conduct a fundraiser and pay
Steinberg up to $11,500 fron the funds raised to pay for the
public opinion poLls conducted by Steinberg on behalf of Friends
of ceorge Hanna. The threshold question, therefore, is whether
l{r. Hanna can use campaign funds to pay the debt incurred for
these public opinion po11s.
Article 8 (commencing lrith Section B58OO) of Section 5 of the
Act describes the pernissible -uses of caupaign funds. Section
85802 (b) states:
_ Canpaign funds shal1 not be used to pay for or
reimburse the cost of professional services-unless
the servj.ces are directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.


Our File No. A-9O-O94
Page 3
Palments for public opinion po11s _ in connection with an
electioir are directly related to a political 
purpose - 
(Section
85802(b).) Therefore, Mr. I{anna may use campaign funds to pay the
debt incurred for the public opinion poI]s.
ltr. Hanna is reguired, to conduct this fund raising effort
within 90 days.2 l,tr.- Ilanna has agreed that if less than $2, ooo is
rai.sed, Mr. ianna will personally nake up the differerlce to
qruar..ri"" a palment ot $z,ooo. Section 85301(b) specifies that
iirit= 
on contiilutions do not apply to a candidate's contribution
of his or her personal funds to his or her own canpaign bank
account. fhus, Ur. Hanna does not violate the Act if he
contributes the $2,OOo to his canpaign account for eventual
oawment to Steinberg. The same rationale lrould apply to Pay the
i.if irrq costs associated with the fund raising effort. 
Pa'nent of
such costs does not violate the Act.
Argruably, since Mr. Steinberg has agreed that he woufd be
willing to aicept less than the fu1l amount of the debt owed to
hG- (ii less thin S11,5oo j.s collected), the difference could be
aE"ria a contribution to George Hanna. However, if a reasonable
settleDent agreement under the circumstances is reached for less
than the amount owed, this difference is not deemed a
contribution. 
(see, steinberq Advice Letter, No' A-86-344r'
Summers Advice Letter, No. A-77-oL4; copies enclosed')
The agreement also provides that if funds are raised in
excess of $tt,soo, steinterq will receive $11,500. steinberg
originally fiifea'the 
comrniltee $9,500. The difference, $2,000'
refiects ihe attorneyrs fees and court costs incurred by Steinbergl
in connection with the lawsuit to collect the debt' J
section 85802(c) provides in relevant part:
Canpaign funds shall not be used to pay or
reimburse f.i-nes, penalties' judqnents, or
settlements, except those resulting frou ... the
f oIlowj.ng:
.,' 
In a telephone conversation on I'tonday, February 5, 1990,
Mr. George Hinna inforroed me that he had not terninated his
candidacy, and that he had not terminated the Friends of George
Hanna controlled cornmittee. Prior to raising any funds, Mr. Hanna
roust file a Form 501 for the 1988 election \./hich he lost. 
In
addition, Mr. Ilanna nust file-a Form 502, listing the account
number of the campaign bank account. As to the date the account
was opened, it would be sufficient to state that it was opened
prior to January 1, 1989.
3 Yor., confirmed this in a telephone conversation on Dlarch 7,
1990.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-094
Page 4
***
Any ... action for which paynent of attorney's
fees from contributions t ould be pernitted pursuant
to this title.
Sectj.on 85802 (c) (2), (enphasis
added).
Section 85802.5 states the general rule in connection with
expenditures for attorney's fees. It essentially provides that
attorney's fees may be paid if the litigation 
arises directly out
of the iandidate's activities, 
duties, or status as a candidate.
In this case, Mr. Hanna could use canpaj.gn funds to pay the
attorney's fees to defend against the lawsuit brought by
steinberg. Therefore, canpaign funds uay be used to pay the
$2,ooo, which reflects the settlement for attorney's fees and
costs in connection wj.th the lawsuit. 
(Section 85802(c) (2).).
Mr. Frank Caternichio, former carnpaign con6ultant of the
comuittee has agreed to supply the co'nrnittee with a list of
contributors. 
For this lttr. Caternichio has agreed to accept as
palment 58 of all sums raised in excess of $11,500. By providinq
ifr-e nailing list to the connittee, if Mr. caternichio charges less
than the fiir 
narket value of the nailing 1j-st, Mr. Caternichio is
deemed to have made a contribution of the difference betvreen the
amount charged by fiIr. Caternichio and the value of the mail-ing
Iist. 
If this amount exceeds $1,ooo, arguably, it would violate
section 85301 which linits contributions to candidates to $1,000
j.n any f iscal year. 4
The provision that aL1 funds would be raised in the name of
the co'nmi-ttee and will be deposited into its checking account
couplies with Section 85201 of the Act. Accordingrly, in
conclusion, the provisions of the settLement agreeuent described
above do not violate the Act.
4  .n a te■ephone conversation on
stated that the fair market va■ue
exceed s■′o00。
Monday, March 5, Mr. Hanna
of the nailing list would not

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, pl.ease contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
